NOT Satisfied with the SERVICE DEPARTMENT; i had my vehicle diagnosed; the technician who started to diagnose the vehicle seemed like he was NOT happy to be part of the service department's team. He either was not experienced or did not think that satisfaction of the customer was important to him. Upon lifting the vehicle, first thing he told me to do is take the car back and have it washed underneath and then bring it back. His superior obviously disagreed with him and recommended to use a cleaner spray to clean the small area which was presenting the problem (a slow leaking oil pipe). From that point onwards till the end of the diagnosis, not only the technician DID NOT use the cleaner spray but also was not able to reach the loose clamp (the culprit of the problem). Throughout the process he did not remove the front wheel which was the right way to be able to reach the clamp easily but was not able to fix a simple clamp. The end of the story is that I had a simple mechanic at a mechanic '"workshop" who charged me a simple Rs. 500 (yes correct 5 hundred and even that I insisted because he was generous enough to resist charging me anything) to fix that clamp and was able to do this on a simple jack by removing the front wheel. At some parts of the world, the dealerships are known as "STEALERSHIPS" for this exact reason. Please step up ur game Honda Gateway and not become a stealership... since your inexperienced technician then proceeded to tell me to have the entire pump replaced until I asked him to get the head mechanic who obviously disagreed with the technician's diagnosis. Even though I was NOT charged by your service department, I still think I have to present an accurate picture on google reviews.
